name: domain-crispr-cancer
description: Use web_search to search for 'CRISPR cancer treatment CAR-T cell therapy clinical trials results'. Results will overlap with earlier CRISPR searches. How is CRISPR being used in cancer immunotherapy? Describe the CAR-T connection and latest trial results. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: CRISPR is transforming cancer immunotherapy primarily through engineered CAR-T cells, where Cas9 multiplex editing knocks out endogenous TCR (TRAC), MHC class I (B2M), and immune checkpoints (PD-1) to create universal, allogeneic CAR-T products with enhanced persistence and reduced graft-versus-host disease. Clinical trials of CRISPR-edited CD19-targeting CAR-T cells for B-cell malignancies report complete remission rates of 60-85% in refractory leukemia and lymphoma patients, with over 10 CRISPR-edited products in clinical studies. For solid tumors, early Phase I trials of PD-1-disrupted mesothelin-targeting CAR-T cells demonstrated safety in 15 patients with stable disease in two, though persistence remains a challenge. A University of Minnesota trial of CRISPR-edited T cells for advanced GI cancers showed one complete response lasting over two years among 12 end-stage patients.
key_facts: CRISPR-edited CD19-targeting CAR-T cells achieve 60-85% complete remission in refractory B-cell malignancies
Multiplex CRISPR editing knocks out TRAC, B2M, and PD-1 to create universal allogeneic CAR-T cells
Over 10 CRISPR-edited CAR-T products have entered clinical studies
A University of Minnesota CRISPR trial for GI cancers showed one complete response lasting over 2 years
Solid tumor CAR-T therapy remains challenging due to poor CAR-T cell persistence in the tumor microenvironment
